Application page now, they fill out a bunch of questions about why we should work with them. Okay, I've gotten myself this, I've got other people like, let me know why you think I should work with you to help you get that result as well. Because I don't want to work with everybody, I want to work with the right people. Why are you the right person to work with me to get that result? Okay, and they're going to go and then sell themselves, they're going to sell you on why they should actually work with you. That's what the application is about. Okay, so the application, a couple of things is, number one, you want to explain what it looks like to work with you. Okay, so if I do happen to pick you, this is going to look like when you call everyone as well, it's going to be a meeting, it's going to be events, whatever it is, the year high end package is, explain what it's going to look like to work with you. We have a full application with them to apply. And inside the application, there's a lot of questions you can ask. When we first started building this, I started looking at every kind of application out there, I was looking at what everybody was doing. And when I found a couple of things, the application questions I asked, I was asking questions like, what type of business do you own? I'm having something like a revenue qualifier. So like, revenue qualifier, I might ask something like, how much money a month do you spend on ads right now? Or how much money do you have to invest in your business? If you're not in a business type situation, maybe you're in fitness or finance, revenue qualifier might be like, how much money do you spend eating out per month? What's your budget for healthy food every single month? So you find out, are they making enough money to actually afford what I'm doing? It sounds like I can't buy healthy food, we can't afford it. Then you have a revenue qualifier knowing this person is probably going to be a big fit for my $30,000 weight loss program, right? Or if they're like, hey, we don't spend any money on Facebook ads because we can't pay the bills, they're not going to be investing my high end program. I'm going to tell them to go buy my book instead or something that they can actually afford at the time. So I have a revenue qualifier. I like asking questions to find out what success looks like for them. One of the big problems you have when you're trying to sell some really expensive things is they come in and you're like, okay, this is our program is going to help you build a funnel. But in their mind, if I sign up for this program, it means I'm going to have a billion dollar funnel, I'm going to have 100,000 leads a day, I'm going to have their vision of successes. And if it's different than what your vision is, what you're actually fulfilling on and there's a discrepancy, no matter how good you are at fulfilling on this thing that you're going to do, if their vision is something over here, you're never going to be successful together. So like asking questions like what does success look like for you? If I decide to work with you and try to help you get these results, what does success look like for you? A lot of times I'll have people who seem like traditional amazing business people and they'll apply for a coaching program with us. Right now I'm making $150,000 a year, which is pretty good, but I want to make sure my take home is no less than a million dollars a year. And then within the next 12 months, I'm like, you know what, the likelihood of you getting a million dollar a year take home after all your expenses and everything is probably not very high, especially in the first year. I don't think I can fulfill on that. So I might say, I'm not going to work with you because your goals are way out of skew. I had one guy, this is a couple of them in mind, he was like, he's doing good, he had a good corporate business and he wanted to make sure that after his advertising costs, all his hard costs and everything, his take home is no less than three to five million dollars a year. And I'm like, to do that, that's awesome, but you've got to build a business like $70, $80, $100 million. We can give you that eventually, but not in the next 12 months. You have to be realistic. So I want to make sure that I understand what success is like with them so I can see, am I going to be able to fulfill that or not? And if I can't fulfill it, I do not want them as a client. So it gives me an idea really quickly to find out if they're a good fit for me. One of the easiest ways to find this out, there's a book called The Question by Dan Sullivan and the book's amazing, but the gist is this is the question. You ask them this question, in fact, if you look at any of my coaching applications, they always ask this. It says, if we were having this discussion three years from today and you're looking back over those last three years, what would have happened into your life both personally and professionally for you to feel happy with the progress? Okay, think about it. I say, look, I just want to have more impact on the world right now. I'm doing well. I'd love to have a company that has at least a million bucks a year. I hope my take home is 30% of that or whatever it is. I'm hoping to be able to blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, when I'm bigger impact on my business, when we're talking to my family, I'm like, boom, I can help you. Those are things that I can help you do very, very easily. So I focus on that. If it's a good fit, that might cool. We're going to be working together. If they're way outside of what's reality, then I'm like, this is probably not going to be a good relationship. And then also, usually when I'm selling coaching, there's some kind of group dynamic. I want to make sure that they're not just takers, but also givers. So I ask, why would you be a good fit for this group? You're going to be working with a lot of other people just like you. Let me know why you're a good fit. And then they sell you and like, why would be a good fit because of this and this and this. It helps to make sure that they're a team player, that they're a giver, not just a taker. And you have to be a good fit for the group. How would you contribute things like that? So those are the types of questions I ask in my application. So again, page number one, case study, page number two, application. The page number three is the homework. Now a couple of things on the homework. Number one is I give them homework that helps build the connection with me. So I might say, hey, go watch this video to tell you the story. A lot of times I have a video to tell about why I got in this business. One of the videos I usually use on my thank you pages is me telling my story about my wife and our kids and how we got, how we couldn't have babies and we got fertility and we ended up having twins and how that doctor that changed my life because of, because of what she was doing. And I tell this whole story, it's emotional. I cry like three or four times in the movie. And the video is kind of embarrassing, but it builds connection with people, right? So I give them homework assignments to go watch trainings and watch things I've done so they can have a better connection with me. Okay. That's number one. And number two is I try to get them to initiate an inbound call. Obviously when the application comes in, I'm going to outbound call them. But we found, back when I had the call center with 60 salespeople, we found that if I was calling them on the phone, back then, the metrics are different today, but we would average about $150 per lead. If I had them to call me, we would average about $450 per lead. Okay, so whatever the math is, like three times more money by getting them to initiate the call. So even though I'm going to call every application that comes in, I'm going to tell them on this page like, hey, if you want to jump to the front of the line, if you're really excited to get started today, like call this number does not mean you're guaranteed a spot at all. It means you're going to jump to the front of the line and you can talk to somebody and see if you're going to fit right now. Okay, and it gets them to initiate an inbound call. And so I try to get them to initiate an inbound call as quick as we can. And so that's kind of what case study phone funnel looks like. Case study, application, homework, where I build connection and get them to initiate a phone call. Okay, and that's it. Very, very simple. Now a variation of case study phone call, instead of a case study of like, let me tell you this case study of how I got myself this result, the next variation is just what we call a success story. Okay, the funnel is very, very similar, but instead of telling a full case study, I'm having someone tell a success story. Okay, so some of you guys have seen this funnel before. This is a video of Liz Benny from page number one. But notice like, this is just me changing the art. The funnel structure is the same, right? The frameworks are all the same. Just here, instead of a case study, I have Liz Benny telling a story and they apply. The page number two, where I have a video telling my story and then they fill out a longer form application. And from there, there's a picture, a video of me telling my story and then trying to get them to create inbound call, okay? So page number one is success story. So this is Liz Benny. Now I'm gonna actually show you guys this video. One thing about when I show you this, I want you to understand, notice that it wasn't like, I worked with Russ when I got rich quick, and income claim, income claim, it's not me telling much income claims and things like that. Okay, as your company grows, you can get a lot of trouble for that. This is her telling her actual success story, okay? And if you watch this video, it's an amazing video. It's made me millions and millions of dollars. But what's interesting is it's not her just talking about how she got rich. It's her talking about how hard it was and how work can be helped during it changed your life. But man, it was this painful process. And what's nice about this, when someone goes to watch this video, when they come into coaching later, they're not like, I thought this was gonna be super easy. I was gonna get rich quick. It's like, no, I'll watch the video. Liz framed this whole coaching program. It's gonna be works, gonna be painful, but man, it's gonna be worth it, right? And generates a different, a better quality lead coming through. So let me show you guys this video. So you can see just an example of a case study video, excuse me, a success story video that we used to fill up a whole coaching program. So I wanna tell you something. Hiring Russell Brunson was, bar none, one of the best decisions I've ever made in my entire life. And I wanna tell you that when you work with him, he takes you on this journey that you won't even know that you're gonna go on. Like, at the very beginning, I had all these hopes and dreams and visions and I honestly thought that, yay, I'm working with the overnight success maker. And I thought, whoa, I'm gonna make like $500,000 in the first month of working with him. But you know what the eventuality is that sometimes in life, it doesn't always work out the exact way that you want it to work out. And that's certainly where it got to for me. So my 2014 was one of the hardest years I've ever had in my entire life. Like, there was cancer in the family, people were in hospital. I thought I was gonna, at one stage, I thought I was gonna lose my partner. Like, it just wasn't a pleasant scenario at all. And by losing my partner, I mean like hospitalization, like really not, like from really bad health issues. And you know, there were just times when I promised you I wanted to give up. And sometimes I actually, when I say that, I don't just mean give up in business. Like, for me, when I hired Russell, it really was like, do, like, sounds really dramatic. But like my life's mission, it's so big. Like I really, really, really wanted to connect with someone who could get me to the next level, who could help me really get from that really stranded, grounded place, you know, sitting at my office every single day. To a place where I'm, you know, helping like people all around the world. And that's why I chose Russell. But man, it was hard. It was really, really, really hard at times to have to deal with those things that come up in your personal life all the time. Like, it was not an easy scenario in 2014, but I'm really, really, really grateful that I listened to Russell. I'm really grateful that I hired him as a coach. Like the man has changed my life forever. Like, you know, once a year, like my mind has been blown completely. And there's no way back. Like I know that anything is possible now. All right. Do you see how powerful that is? The success story of someone who's gone through your process. And you share that. And again, it's not tons of income claims and things like that or, you know, weight loss claim, those things, just them telling their story and their journey. And the right people are going to hear that and they're going to come and they're going to come into your funnels. Okay. And so that's what the success story funnel looks like. Again, a video, they're going to watch a video showing a success story and then they're going to apply if they want your help achieving that same result. Okay. And then again, same thing on the page they apply. They get email address so you can follow them if they don't fill out the application. And page number two then, we're taking this page right here. There's a video and in the video I explain what it looks like to work with me. I have a whole bunch of different testimonials scrolling on that video while they're filling out the longer form application. And then from there goes page number three. There's the video right here I was talking about where me telling my story about my wife and the twins. I cry like three times on this video. They watch this video to build connection. And then again, I tell them to try to initiate an inbound call so it increases the chance and likely they're going to be joining our coaching programs. Okay. Okay. So that's the funnels. They're very simple. They're very easy. They're not complicated at all. I got this, I got this lead. It's amazing. What do I do with it? Okay. And so I want to, I want to step back really quick before I go too deep into this. I want you to start thinking about for any kind of business like whenever people come to me like, well, my business is different because we don't sell things online and we don't whatever like this funnel right here. Do you see how this funnel, how it preframes and it structures and it creates like the perfect dream lead for you. Right. You get somebody to watch a case study of something you've done for yourself or for somebody else or a success story where they have this amazing frame. And they give you the email address and they go to the next page and they're telling you like why you should work and they're selling themselves. And you're like, you should pick me because of this, because of this and this is what success looks like and this is why do you get to contribute to the group and they're selling themselves on why you should pick them. Okay. Then you get the lead. Now imagine any kind of business that's got any kind of phone sales team, right? Now they're outbound calling leads. They're calling cold calling people that are getting, you know, buying leads offline or whatever it is, like calling that kind of lead versus calling this kind of lead where somebody is amazing and they're prepared and they're ready to work with you and they're telling you why you should pick them. Like it shifts everything. We went from us outbound calling leads averaging $150 DPL per lead to we shifted to this and now we average over $1,500 per application, 10 times as much money by taking them through this process. Okay. Again, I went from 60 sales guys to two, making the exact same amount of revenue. Okay. Do you see the power behind that? Like that's what this process does. It generates and creates the most amazing dream lead you can dream of. Okay. So even if you're not selling coaching, it doesn't matter if you're selling anything where you're calling leads on the phone. If you've taken through this process, you're washing the people through this process to prepare them to indoctrinate them to warm them up and get them selling you on why you should allow them to buy your prior to your service. Like it changes everything. Do you see that? Like so anytime I'm working with a company and they're like, why do you need to lead them? Like you should use a phone funnel. Like why don't you sell coaching? Like it doesn't matter if you're selling coaching. Okay. If you're calling people on the phone and trying to sell anything, man take them through this process initially and that the lead becomes worth 10 times as much money to you or more. Okay. So the one amazing thing is instead of having 60 salespeople, I'm only getting the cream of the crop. The best people are coming through and the best leads come through. Okay. I'm not just wasting all this energy with 60 people calling everybody trying to find out who's actually ready. The funnel is all the work, the sifting, the sorting, the preparation, the pre-work, the pre-swage, like all those things happen and we're just giving this amazing piece of gold at the end. Okay. All right. So now we have this goal. Now the question is like, what do we say on the phone? Okay. Now again, this is outside of the context of this training. We have a product called High-Ticket Secrets that goes deep into this. We also inside the dot-com Secrets book, I go deep into both of the different phone scripts. I'm going to kind of go through them really quickly here. There's two scripts. Number one is what we call the four question clothes. Okay. The four question clothes works if you don't have a salesperson. It's just you. Okay. Or maybe you have one salesperson. Okay. The application comes in and there's these four questions that we go through. Okay. Again, question number one, notice it's very similar to the application. Question number one is all about, hey, if we were to have this conversation in three years from now, we're looking back last year, what would have had happened both professionally and personally and professionally for you to feel like this was successful? Okay. And they come back and they start dreaming about the future and visualizing what's going to be possible. Okay. And if they're asking, okay, well, what's keeping you back right now? Like, why are you hitting that? Like, well, I'm not doing it because I'm stuck here. I'm stuck here. They're talking about the things that's keeping them from the results that they want. And you say, okay, based on that, like, what are the resources you have that you haven't tapped into yet? Like, tell me about what you have. And they're like, well, you know, I have an email list, I don't really email, I've got this, I've got this. They're telling you all the things that they have through the untapped resources so you know exactly how to work with them. They say, cool. And then the next question is like, well, do you think based on what you know of me so far, do you think that I can help you get that goal? Right? Like, I see where you're trying to go. I see where you're stuck. I know resources you have. I think that I can help you get there. Would you like my help with that? Typically, I say yes. And they're like, cool. Well, let's see you signed up. Okay. So that's what goes into more detail about this. I'm not a phone sales person so my team is the ones who develop these questions and they go deep into it. So if you read the dot com secrets book, it'll go deeper. But this is the four question close. Okay. So if you got one sales person, this is the script we use. Now the other thing we do is we, for most of our clients, we try to set up both a setter and a closer. Okay. If you do that, the script's a little bit different. And I'm not going to go deep into the script because it's, again, it'll take me a while to go into it. And I don't know as well as the sales people. But in, again, the dot com secrets book, it's all in there. Basically, there's two scripts. Number one is the setter. The setter is the first person talks and gets on the phone. And the setter's job is to qualify them to see if they're actually a good fit for your program. So they go through these first eight parts. They have the intro, they ask questions, they go through the blasts, the posture, the probe, the finding credit, the goals and the commitments, find out if they are a good fit. Okay. This way that person is going through finding out if they're a good fit. If they are a good fit, then the person hands them off to the closer and the closer goes to the next set of questions to figure out how to get them in the program, make sure that everything's going to work out good. Okay. So it's a two person setter, a closer script. This is the best script when you're not doing any of the selling. Okay. Told you before that I'm scared to death of getting on the phone. I'm very introverted. I didn't want to do these kinds of things. And so when we got started, I had setter and a closer and it gave the ability to position a posture me. Okay. With the four question script back here, a lot of times it's the guru. It's you on the phone doing the cell. That's why we do the four question close. Okay. But at the same time, if there's always a decrease in positioning when you're the one doing the call, right? If you're also the one who's doing the coaching or whatever the thing at the end, you're like, you have time to do a phone call. Like it kind of depositioned you. But a lot of times this is where people start and then as they grow a little bit better and they step away from the sales role, they used to shift to this where the setter and the closer are all that edifying you and posturing and positioning you. Okay. The way the script works, it makes, it increases your intrinsic value by the way that the script works. And then when someone's done and they go through this process, then they come to you. So for me, when we started this new sales process, again, I didn't want to be on the phones. I'm scared to death of being on the phone. I set a closer and did tens of millions of dollars in sales. So I'm going to watch this video. They'd apply. The setter would get on the phone. So if they're going to fit, it's so pass them off to the closer, closer, close the sale and then they'd have a chance to come work with me and I was to be able to help them at that level. Okay. So that's kind of the process. But again, these funnels are very simple. Okay. Either case study of what you've done or how you've done for others or success story. They watch that. They give the email address to work with you. Number two is they will go through a full long form application where they're selling you on why you should work with them. Number three, you give them homework to build connection and you have to try to create an inbound call. And then during that inbound call or when you outbound call them, then you either if you're, if you're one person sales team, you go through the four question clothes. If you're two sell person sales team, then you go through the setter closer script and that's it. That's the magic how tight ticket funnels work. The cool thing about high ticket funnels like this is it gives you the ability to sell things at all sorts of levels. Okay. We first started doing this back, man, a decade ago. We were selling things for $5,000. But I thought it was like, that's so much money. No one ever buy anything besides five. Everyone after we shut down the big call center, we opened the small two person call center initially we started selling things at $8,000. And it blew my mind that the scripts worked. So you get people to buy eight and we raised the prices to 10, from 10 to 12 and then 12 to 15, 15 to 25 and 20 to five to 50 and beyond. And what we found is these scripts work at every single price point. We've got people now who are selling a hundred thousand dollars offers with a million dollar offers within it. It works all throughout. So if you are trying to sell high ticket stuff, it is, it is the key is the goal is the secret.
